title: Support cue copy and visibility phase
summary: Lane 2 should prepare and implement one tiny handheld-safe cue that helps players notice when active support is biasing live clue finding.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the compact support-cue copy and icon handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The route now plays differently, but that difference is still easier to prove than to feel at a glance.

execution_order.goal: Implement one tiny cue in an existing seam.
execution_order.why_now: A small cue can surface the support win without growing a new HUD.

execution_order.goal: Review the cue for fit and restraint.
execution_order.why_now: The cue only helps if it stays calmer than the rest of the route shell.

guardrails: Use an existing seam instead of inventing a new HUD lane.
Keep copy shorter than a route strip or notebook card.
Prefer one icon or one two-word cue over explanatory prose.

candidate_seams: an already-live route strip label with a stronger support-shaped state

gating: only when `prefersHandLensActiveEntry(...)` is true for the inspected entry
leave generic notebook-fit bubbles on the calmer existing label path
